goal: Make Josh 2.0 Telegram the iPhone-first command center with tap-first controls and clear activity visibility.

inlineKeyboards.rule: Use single-column rows for important choices and max two buttons per row for compact secondary actions.

callbackQueries.rule: Every callback must answerCallbackQuery quickly so the Telegram spinner clears.

replyKeyboard.rule: Reserve persistent reply keyboards for a future dedicated control-panel mode. Inline buttons are less intrusive for normal chat.

webApp.rule: Use when Mission Control has a stable HTTPS URL; local file is ready and dashboard-safe.

copyTextButtons.rule: Use for commands, one-time codes, URLs, or handoff snippets that Josh may need to paste elsewhere.

forceReply.rule: Use for step-by-step forms when Josh must type a specific answer.

editableWorkCards.rule: Use scripts/josh_work_card.py for any user-requested task with more than one step, any task likely to exceed about 60 seconds, or any task that changes Mission Control/agent state.

overviewDigest.rule: Use scripts/josh_telegram_digest.py overview|daily for compact ecosystem summaries.

agentQuickCards.rule: Use scripts/josh_agent_quick_card.py <agent> or agent:<agent> callbacks for compact agent-specific status cards.

rules: Start with one plain Active Model/Auth line.
Leave one blank line after the model/auth line.
Use short hyphen bullets for Route, Objective, Status, Now, Done, Blocker, and Next.
Keep each bullet to one short mobile-readable sentence when possible.
Prefer semicolons or short fragments over long wrapped paragraphs.
Do not use dense paragraph labels for every field.
statusTemplate.example:
Codex 5.5 (openai-codex subscription)

- Route: Josh 2.0 direct chat; foreground lane.
- Objective: Test Telegram readability.
- Status: Good; responsive.
- Now: Using compact bullets.
- Done: Brain Feed hook fired; direct chat path works.
- Blocker: None.
- Next: Tap a button or send the next task.
